redirects._comment: QR-stable yard sign link. /yardsign is printed on physical yard signs with a QR code; this redirect points scanners to the current GBP listing. 302 (not 301) on purpose: the destination is meant to be swappable without re-printing signs, and 301 would let browsers cache an old target. To repoint: change `destination` below and redeploy.

QR-stable 'leave a review' link for print tracking pieces (card-back, sticker). Lands directly on the Google review form so scanners can rate without an extra tap. Same 302-not-301 rationale as /yardsign: destination must stay swappable. UTM query strings (utm_source/medium/campaign) pass through to the destination automatically, so we can see in Google's referrer + any downstream tooling which printed piece drove the scan. We deliberately use /r (not /review) because /review is the canonical reviews landing page (live Google Reviews widget + schema.org aggregateRating). Don't merge them.

Missing hub pages. 16 location-variant hail-damage pages and 16 storm-damage pages link to /services/hail-damage and /services/storm-damage-insurance-claim respectively (parent breadcrumbs). Neither URL has a backing HTML file; both 404'd. The canonical insurance-claim landing is /services/hail-damage-insurance-claim (exists). The canonical storm-damage landing is /services/storm-damage (exists). Use 301 because these URLs are semantically permanent aliases — search engines should fold the link equity into the canonical URLs.

/free-guide is a CONTRACTOR lead magnet (not homeowner-facing). Relocated to the SaaS/contractor surface at /sites/free-guide. 301 so any printed/ad/QR links to the old /free-guide fold their equity into the new location.
/financing (root) was a client-side meta-refresh stub to the canonical /services/financing. Replaced with a proper server 301 (better SEO, no redirect flash); the stub file was removed.

rewrites._comment: Bots, link previewers, and old browsers probe /favicon.ico by reflex. We don't ship a .ico file (favicon.svg covers modern browsers via <link rel=icon>). Rewriting the .ico URL to favicon.svg returns the SVG bytes with Content-Type image/svg+xml — modern browsers accept that fine. The alternative was a 404 every time a crawler hits the URL.

Server-rendered preview for portal share links. The function reads the token off req.path, validates it, and renders an HTML page with og: meta so messaging apps show a real preview card. See functions/share-ssr.js.

headers._comment: Wave 127 P0: JS/CSS must-revalidate on every request via ETag. Previously max-age=300 meant the browser HTTP cache served stale assets for 5 minutes after each deploy — even with the W124 network-first Service Worker, the SW's fetch() would hit browser HTTP cache and get stale code. Symptom: 'every other reload stuck on loading' because each load's background HTTP-cache update set up the NEXT load to work, then a deploy could break it again. New: max-age=0 + must-revalidate makes the browser send If-None-Match (ETag) on every request. 99% of the time the file is unchanged and the server returns 304 Not Modified (~5KB total, ~50ms). When it does change (deploy), the user gets fresh content immediately. CDN libraries on unpkg/cdnjs are still cache-first via the SW because they're version-pinned in the URL path itself.

Source is '**' not '**/*.html' because cleanUrls:true rewrites incoming /pro/foo.html requests to /pro/foo BEFORE header matching runs. With **/*.html only the JS/CSS rule above matched anything; HTML responses got HSTS only. '**' applies these headers to every response. CSP/X-Frame are no-ops on non-HTML responses (browsers ignore), so the over-application is safe; Cache-Control is overridden per-path below for dashboard/customer/etc.

headers._comment: Sources below use canonical (no-.html) form because cleanUrls:true rewrites incoming /foo.html requests to /foo BEFORE header matching. Sources of the form '/path.html' never match; canonical '/path' matches both forms. Last-match wins for headers (verified prod: /admin/login → no-store from /admin/** rule), so these path-specific rules correctly override the '**' default above.

Public brand assets (logo, illustrations) must be embeddable cross-origin. The Universal Doc Viewer (docs/pro/js/nbd-doc-viewer.js) renders generated PDFs inside <iframe srcdoc> *without* `allow-same-origin` — that gives the iframe a `null` origin, and the global Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin above refuses to serve these images into it, leaving every generated document with a broken-image placeholder where the company logo should be. These are public marketing assets — letting them embed cross-origin is safe (CORP only governs *embedding*; it does not grant script-level read access). Targeted at /assets/images/** specifically so unrelated assets keep the stricter default. Placed AFTER the ** rule so last-match-wins lets this one stick.

Wave 127 P0: the actual Service Worker lives at /pro/sw.js (registered with scope /pro/), but the rule above only matches /sw.js. Result: prod was serving the SW with the default max-age=300 — meaning Cloudflare + browser cached the SW for 5 minutes, blocking SW updates from reaching users for that window. Previous deploys' SW updates (W124, etc.) were stuck in cache. This rule fixes the SW path explicitly. The SW itself MUST never be cached for any meaningful duration — its job is to detect updates, and if the update-detection path is cached, every fix to the SW takes 5+ minutes to propagate per CDN edge.

FIXME(csp): customer.html still has inline <script> blocks (incl. the critical module at line 2710 that runs Firebase init + onAuthStateChanged + loadCustomerData). The strict ** CSP at line 44 blocks them and the page hangs on 'Loading...' forever. Re-allow 'unsafe-inline' for script-src JUST for /pro/customer until customer.html's inline scripts are extracted to external .js files (mirror of #398 dashboard.html work). Last-match-wins per the line-50 comment, so this overrides the ** block CSP for this route only.

headers._comment: AI TOOLS iframe embedding — dashboard.html embeds these 4 pages as same-origin iframes (see <template id="tpl-view-aitree">, etc. in dashboard.html). The global ** rule sets X-Frame-Options: DENY + CSP frame-ancestors 'none' which blocks ALL framing — every AI TOOLS tab in the dashboard renders as 'refused to display in a frame'. These per-route overrides relax framing to same-origin only (X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN + frame-ancestors 'self') so the dashboard can embed them while still blocking cross-origin clickjacking. Canonical paths (no .html) — cleanUrls strips .html before header matching per the line-50 comment, so the dead /pro/{ai-tree,understand,analytics}.html rules above never fire.

_comment_emulators: Local QA / test-only emulator suite (Audit #3). Read ONLY by `firebase emulators:*`; zero effect on production hosting or deploys. Hosts pinned to 127.0.0.1 because this container's IPv6 loopback (::1) is unsupported (EAFNOSUPPORT) and otherwise spams port-check warnings. singleProjectMode keeps every emulated service under one project id so seed scripts and the client SDK agree on tenancy.
